steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: The sentence before the underlined portion defines reduplication in abstract terms: an element of a root word is repeated, sometimes with modification, within a related word. The underlined part immediately follows and demonstrates that concept using the specific Hoocak words already introduced.

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — ‘It elaborates on the description of reduplication by exemplifying how it works in the specific Hoocak word mentioned earlier.’: The underlined portion identifies the repeated element (‘ye’) and shows exactly where it appears in both Hoocak words, turning the abstract definition into a concrete example. ✓
  • Choice B — ‘It provides English translation of the Hoocak word mentioned earlier.’: The English meanings (‘bad’ and ‘bad houses’) were given earlier in the sentence, not in the underlined portion. ✗
  • Choice C — ‘It acknowledges that Hoocak has some important exceptions to the general pattern described earlier.’: The underlined portion confirms the pattern works; it does not introduce exceptions. ✗
  • Choice D — ‘It emphasizes how frequently reduplication occurs in Hoocak.’: The passage says nothing about the frequency of reduplication. ✗
That matches choice A — A is correct.
